perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that; (a) it will use Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of discussing and evaluating the Transaction, and for no other purpose; 1 (b) it will not distribute, disclose or disseminate Information to any third Party without the prior consent of the Disclosing Party, and will only distribute, disclose or disseminate Information to its directors, officers, employees consultants, advisors, potential sources of financing or other representatives with a need to know such information for purposes of discussing and evaluating the Transaction and who are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information; (c) it will protect the Information of the Disclosing Part), against unauthorized disclosure and warrants that will use the same means to protect Information that it uses to protect its own confidential information, in any event no less than reasonable means.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that; (a) it will use Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of discussing and evaluating the Transaction, and for no other purpose; 1 (b) it *will* distribute, disclose or disseminate Information to any third Party without the prior consent of the Disclosing Party, and will only distribute, disclose or disseminate Information to its directors, officers, employees consultants, advisors, potential sources of financing or other representatives with a need to know such information for purposes of discussing and evaluating the Transaction and who are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information; (c) it will protect the Information of the Disclosing Part), against unauthorized disclosure and warrants that will use the same means to protect Information that it uses to protect its own confidential information, in any event no less than reasonable means.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will not' to 'will' in clause 2(b), it creates a direct contradiction. Initially, the Receiving Party was prohibited from distributing Information to third parties without consent. The edited clause now states that they *will* distribute the information without consent. This makes the Receiving party required to disseminate the Information without consent from the Disclosing party, therefore it's a clear in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that; (a) it will use Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of discussing and evaluating the Transaction, and for no other purpose; 1 (b) it *will* distribute, disclose or disseminate Information to any third Party without the prior consent of the Disclosing Party, and will only distribute, disclose or disseminate Information to its directors, officers, employees consultants, advisors, potential sources of financing or other representatives with a need to know such information for purposes of discussing and evaluating the Transaction and who are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information; (c) it will protect the Information of the Disclosing Part), against unauthorized disclosure and warrants that will use the same means to protect Information that it uses to protect its own confidential information, in any event no less than reasonable means.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will not' to 'will' in clause (b) directly contradicts the original intent and the purpose of the non-disclosure agreement. The original text prevents the Receiving Party from disseminating information, while the changed text mandates dissemination, thus creating a direct and irreconcilable conflict. As the explanation states, 'it makes the Receiving party required to disseminate the Information without consent from the Disclosing party, therefore it's a clear in-text contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: Vector agrees that, for a period of three (3) months from the termination or expiration of this Agreement, unless such shall have been specifically invited in writing by SafeNet, neither Vector nor any of the investment funds it manages will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect to seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of SafeNet; (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving SafeNet; (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to SafeNet; or (iv) any “solicitation” or “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of SafeNet; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the Securities Exchange Act) relating to SafeNet or its securities; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert. with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of SafeNet; (d) take any action which might force SafeNet to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (e) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: Vector agrees that, for a period of three (3) months from the termination or expiration of this Agreement, unless such shall have been specifically invited in writing by SafeNet, neither Vector nor any of the investment funds it manages *will* in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect to seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of SafeNet; (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving SafeNet; (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to SafeNet; or (iv) any “solicitation” or “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of SafeNet; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the Securities Exchange Act) relating to SafeNet or its securities; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert. with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of SafeNet; (d) take any action which might force SafeNet to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (e) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 4, Changing 'will not' to 'will' implies that Vector *is* required to perform actions like acquiring securities, making tender offers, or soliciting proxies related to SafeNet, contradicting the original intention of preventing such actions. This creates a clear contradiction within the same section, as it initially restricts Vector's activities but then mandates them due to the change of one word. The legal implication would now force Vector to take actions they were previously restricted from doing, causing a legal conflict of actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vector agrees that, for a period of three (3) months from the termination or expiration of this Agreement, unless such shall have been specifically invited in writing by SafeNet, neither Vector nor any of the investment funds it manages *will* in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect to seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of SafeNet; (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving SafeNet; (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to SafeNet; or (iv) any “solicitation” or “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of SafeNet; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the Securities Exchange Act) relating to SafeNet or its securities; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert. with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of SafeNet; (d) take any action which might force SafeNet to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (e) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will not' to 'will' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement, which was to restrict Vector from engaging in specific activities related to SafeNet. Now, the Changed Text mandates Vector to perform those same activities. This is a clear and direct conflict within the same section, making the agreement's enforcement unclear and creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the explanation provided. The Contradicted Text further emphasizes the effect of this change, as it now requires Vector to do the opposite of what it was initially supposed to do.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party represents that, as of the date of this agreement, neither Party nor its controlled affiliates, nor anyone acting on its or their behalf in connection with the Transaction, has acquired record or beneficial ownership of any voting securities of the other Party or of any securities convertible or exchangeable into or exercisable for voting securities of the other Party, and each Party agrees that neither it, nor any of its directors, officers or employees who have been provided with the Information will perform any purchase, sale or other transfer of any of the securities of the other Party in violation of United States securities laws.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party represents that, as of the date of this agreement, *each* Party nor its controlled affiliates, nor anyone acting on its or their behalf in connection with the Transaction, has acquired record or beneficial ownership of any voting securities of the other Party or of any securities convertible or exchangeable into or exercisable for voting securities of the other Party, and each Party agrees that neither it, nor any of its directors, officers or employees who have been provided with the Information will perform any purchase, sale or other transfer of any of the securities of the other Party in violation of United States securities laws.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'neither' to 'each' it contradicts with the first part of the statement. Initially, the first part states 'neither Party... has acquired any voting securities'. However, by changing this 'each Party nor...' creates an in-text contradiction where each party *has* acquired, but the original clause initially stated that *neither* has. It creates uncertainty about the security ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party represents that, as of the date of this agreement, *each* Party nor its controlled affiliates, nor anyone acting on its or their behalf in connection with the Transaction, has acquired record or beneficial ownership of any voting securities of the other Party or of any securities convertible or exchangeable into or exercisable for voting securities of the other Party, and each Party agrees that neither it, nor any of its directors, officers or employees who have been provided with the Information will perform any purchase, sale or other transfer of any of the securities of the other Party in violation of United States securities laws.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'neither' with 'each', creating a direct contradiction. The original text states that 'neither Party' has acquired securities, while the changed text implies that 'each Party' has acquired securities. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and so it does create a contradiction based on the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation or legal process to disclose any of the Disclosing Party’s Information, the Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly before such Information is disclosed so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the sole reasonable discretion of the Disclosing Party, waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation or legal process to disclose any of the Disclosing Party’s Information, the Receiving Party *will not* notify the Disclosing Party promptly before such Information is disclosed so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the sole reasonable discretion of the Disclosing Party, waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 7, the Receiving Party has an obligation to notify the Disclosing Party of any legal requests to disclose information before the disclosure occurs. By changing 'will notify' to 'will not notify' creates an in-text contradiction. This undermines the Disclosing Party's right to seek protection of its confidential information. This leads to the Receiving party breaching the terms of the agreement, due to this change, and the Disclosing Party is unable to exercise their right to remedy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation or legal process to disclose any of the Disclosing Party’s Information, the Receiving Party *will not* notify the Disclosing Party promptly before such Information is disclosed so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the sole reasonable discretion of the Disclosing Party, waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party 'will notify' the Disclosing Party, while the changed text states the Receiving Party 'will not notify' the Disclosing Party, and the contradicted text contains the same statement as the changed text: 'will not notify'. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency because one version imposes a duty to notify, while the other explicitly prohibits it. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party. The explanation provided further clarifies that the contradiction undermines the Disclosing Party's right to seek protection, leading to a breach of agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that any proceeding relating to this Agreement shall be brought in a state court of Maryland or a court of the United States of America located in the State of Maryland. Each Party hereby consents to personal jurisdiction in any such action, consent to service of process by mail and waive any objection to venue in any such court or to any claim that such court is an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that any proceeding relating to this Agreement shall be brought in a state court of Maryland or a court of the United States of America located in the State of Maryland. Each Party *does not consent* to personal jurisdiction in any such action, consent to service of process by mail and waive any objection to venue in any such court or to any claim that such court is an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.explanation: By inserting 'does not consent' this creates an in-text contradiction of consenting for the personal jurisdiction, with the same clause it does not consent to personal jurisdiction. The first part of the sentence states that the agreement will be brought in Maryland, however it then says that the consent for personal jurisdiction will be removed, thus contradicting the jurisdiction agreement. If it is the case, due to the edit made, the Disclosing party and the Receiving Party, might not be able to proceed the legal proceeding
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that any proceeding relating to this Agreement shall be brought in a state court of Maryland or a court of the United States of America located in the State of Maryland. Each Party *does not consent* to personal jurisdiction in any such action, consent to service of process by mail and waive any objection to venue in any such court or to any claim that such court is an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct in-text contradiction. The original text states that each party consents to personal jurisdiction in Maryland. The changed text explicitly states that each party 'does not consent' to personal jurisdiction in the same sentence and context. This directly contradicts the establishment of jurisdiction, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, aligning with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
